at similar counts is not).

ordering effects). Validate candidate rules against the whole dataset and report accuracy; if no
rule reaches 100%, characterize the residual precisely. You may also probe the live oracle
(hs_server.sh — remember the --port= workaround) with crafted theories to isolate boundaries.

1	# Clean-room reimplementation protocol — tamarin-rs relicensing
2	
3	Purpose: independently recreate three modules of tamarin-rs (wellformedness
4	checker, graph/dot pipeline, web layer) without access to GPL-covered
5	expression, so the results are original works relicensable at will.
6	
7	## Rooms
8	- DIRTY ROOM (Kevin Milner + main-session assistant): has read the GPL Haskell
9	  source and the ported Rust modules. Produces only: interoperability headers
10	  (type surfaces, entry-point signatures, comments stripped), oracle wrappers,
11	  and behavioral bug reports. Never contributes implementation text.
12	- CLEAN ROOM (isolated agents, one per cluster): receive ONLY the materials
13	  listed per cluster below. Their complete tool-call transcripts are retained
14	  as evidence of non-access.
15	
16	## Clean-room materials (exhaustive)
17	1. This protocol, the cluster SPEC.md, and interface/ headers (type surfaces +
18	   boundary signatures only).
19	2. Black-box oracles: `wf_oracle.sh` (runs the compiled Haskell binary on an
20	   input file), `hs_server.sh` (serves a theory in the Haskell web UI for HTTP
21	   probing), pre-captured oracle outputs (oracle/captures/,
22	   oracle/captured_responses/), and GPL example .spthy files used solely as
23	   observation INPUTS.
24	3. Public Rust toolchain and general documentation.
25	
26	## Forbidden to the clean room
27	- Reading ANY file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ — source, comments, git
28	  history — with the sole exceptions of executing the oracle scripts and
29	  reading oracle/captured_responses/ (captured program OUTPUT, not source).
30	- Fetching tamarin-prover source, docs describing its internals, or its GitHub.
31	- Reproducing any tamarin-prover implementation detail from model memory:
32	  every behavior must be derived from the oracle, the captures, or the SPEC.
33	  Exact output strings (warning texts, DOT syntax, HTML) MUST be taken from
34	  observed oracle output — that is compatibility content, not expression.
35	
36	## Audit & integration (after implementation)
37	1. Transcript audit: verify from the retained agent transcripts that no
38	   forbidden path was accessed.
39	2. Similarity audit: a reviewer (may see both sides) compares the clean code
40	   against the Haskell originals; flagged similarities are sent back as
41	   redo-instructions, never patched directly.
42	3. Behavioral acceptance: byte-parity corpus must pass.
43	4. Integration: dirty room may write thin adapters and mechanical renames to
44	   fit the workspace API, but must not transplant logic from the replaced
45	   modules. The replaced modules are deleted; their GPL-pending headers go
46	   with them (history retains them under GPL).

content.content: 1 # BEHAVIOR.md — observed spec of the tamarin web-UI constraint-system graph payload 2 3 All statements below are derived from OBSERVED oracle output: the 81 captured 4 crawl manifests under `oracle/captured_responses/` (14705 graph payloads) and 5 targeted live probing of the black-box HS binary (see QUERIES.log). No 6 tamarin-prover source was read. Where a behavior could not be pinned down 7 black-box it is marked **[GAP]**. 8 9 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 10 ## 1. Transport / payload family 11 12 A crawl manifest is JSON: `{base, lemmas, log, capped, manifest}` where 13 `manifest` maps a URL to `{kind, status, body}` (kind ∈ html|text|json|dot). 14 15 Graph-bearing URL families (theory index `N` replaces `#` in captures): 16 17 | URL family | kind | payload | 18 |---|---|---| 19 | `/thy/trace/N/interactive-graph-def/proof/[/...]` | `dot` | **the graph** — graphviz DOT text (`text/plain`) | 20 | `/thy/trace/N/intdot/proof/[/...]` | `html` | wrapper page embedding `` | 21 | `/thy/trace/N/main/cases/{raw,refined}/i/j` | `json` | `{html,title}`; html contains `` | 22 23 The DOT text at `interactive-graph-def` is the whole deliverable. `intdot` is a 24 fixed HTML shell (only the `` and the `dotsrc` path vary). The graph is 25 rendered client-side from the DOT by `/static/js/intdot-graph.es.js`. 26 27 **JSON graph payload** [GAP]: the binary exposes a *separate* `--with-json` 28 rendering backend (`tamarin-prover interactive --help`). No JSON rendering tool 29 is installed here and no `interactive-graph-def` body in the corpus is JSON — 30 the entire corpus is the DOT backend. JSON graph format is therefore unobserved. 31 32 **Graph options** [GAP]: query params on the graph URL 33 (`?simplification=`, `?abbreviate=`, `?level=`, `?compress=`, `?simplify=`, …) 34 produced byte-identical output; there is no per-request simplification-level 35 knob, and no CLI simplification-level flag. See §5. 36 37 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 38 ## 2. DOT document structure 39 40 Two headers occur, selected by a single rule (§4): 41 42 ### 2a. Simple header (non-clustered) 43 ``` 44 digraph "G" { 45 nodesep="0.3"; 46 ranksep="0.3"; 47 node[fontsize="8",fontname="Helvetica",width="0.3",height="0.2"]; 48 edge[fontsize="8",fontname="Helvetica"]; 49 <statements…> 50 51 } 52 ``` 53 54 ### 2b. Compact header (clustered) 55 ``` 56 digraph "G" { 57 nodesep="0.8"; 58 ranksep="0.8"; 59 sep="4"; 60 splines="true"; 61 overlap="false"; 62 pack="true"; 63 packmode="cluster"; 64 concentrate="true"; 65 compound="true"; 66 remincross="true"; 67 mclimit="10"; 68 nslimit="20"; 69 nslimit1="20"; 70 ordering="out"; 71 rankdir="TB"; 72 showboxes="false"; 73 clusterrank="local"; 74 node[fontsize="8",fontname="Helvetica",width="0.3",height="0.2",margin="0.05,0.05",shape="ellipse"]; 75 edge[fontsize="8",fontname="Helvetica",penwidth="1.5",arrowsize="0.5",color="black",style="solid",weight="8"]; 76 <statements…> 77 78 } 79 ``` 80 81 ### Block / whitespace rule (byte-exact) 82 Every block (`digraph … {`, `subgraph … {`, anonymous `{`) is emitted as: 83 `OPEN "\n"` then each inner statement as `STMT "\n"`, then one blank line, then 84 `}`. i.e. the body always ends `…\n\n}`. The top-level digraph is followed by a 85 final `\n`. Subgraph/anonymous blocks are themselves statements of their parent 86 (so the parent supplies the `\n` after their closing `}`). There are **no** blank 87 lines between sections (header attrs / nodes / edges / subgraphs run together); 88 the only blank line in each block is the one before its own `}`. 89 An empty graph is header attrs + blank + `}` (154 bytes). 90 91 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 92 ## 3. Node & edge syntax 93 94 Only three node shapes appear in proof graphs: `record`, `ellipse`, `plain`. 95 96 ### 3a. Record node (a rule / graph-node instance) 97 ``` 98 n<ID>[shape="record",label="{{<p> cell|<p> cell}|{<p> cell}|{<p> cell|<p> cell}}",fillcolor="#RRGGBB",style="filled",fontcolor="black|white",role="<Role>"]; 99 ``` 100 - Attribute order is fixed: `shape,label,fillcolor,style,fontcolor,role`. 101 - Label is a graphviz record: three top groups `{prem}|{info}|{concl}` (a group 102 may be absent/empty). Each group = cells joined by `|`. Each cell = 103 `<portid> text` (port id like `n0`, a space, then the fact text). Ports are 104 numbered per-graph; a record's own node id is allocated AFTER its port ids. 105 - Record-label text escaping: `<`→`\<`, `>`→`\>`, literal `{ } |`→`\{ \} \|`; 106 long facts are wrapped with `\l` (left-justified break) and indented with 107 ` ` runs. (The exact wrap width / term pretty-printing is **[GAP]** — it 108 is graphviz-layout / solver-term-renderer dependent; the model treats cell 109 text as pre-rendered.) 110 - `fillcolor` is a deterministic per-rule color (same rule name ⇒ same color 111 within a graph). `role="Undefined"` for plain multiset-rewriting rules; 112 otherwise the process/agent role. `fontcolor` is `black` on the light MSR 113 palette, `white` on the saturated per-role cluster palette. Exact color hash 114 is **[GAP]**; the model carries colors explicitly. 115 116 ### 3b. Ellipse node (atomic / knowledge / action / temporal node) 117 ``` 118 n<ID>[label="<text>",shape="ellipse"]; 119 n<ID>[label="<text>",shape="ellipse",color="<c>"]; 120 ``` 121 - Attribute order: `label,shape[,color]`. Optional `color` (`gray` for `!KU` 122 intruder-knowledge nodes, `darkblue` for action/event nodes, `gray30`, …). 123 - Ellipse text is NOT record-escaped (`<`,`>` appear literally, e.g. 124 `Session( 'I', <pk(~ltkA), pkB>, … )`). 125 126 ### 3c. Edge 127 ``` 128 n<S>[:<sport>] -> n<D>[:<dport>][<attrs>]; 129 ``` 130 Endpoints optionally carry a `:port` (record port). `<attrs>` is a 131 comma-separated attribute list, in observed key order. Frequent styles: 132 133 | attrs | meaning (inferred from context) | 134 |---|---| 135 | `color="red",style="dashed"` | intruder-deduction / `!KU` edge | 136 | `style="bold",weight="10.0",color="gray50"` | structural premise/conclusion edge | 137 | `style="bold",weight="10.0"` | structural edge (uncolored) | 138 | `color="gray30"` | message/std edge | 139 | `color="blue3",style="dashed"` | temporal-order edge | 140 | `color="orangered2"` | (deduction variant) | 141 | `color="black",style="dashed"` | before/less-than temporal edge | 142 | `style="invis"` | ranking edge to the legend node | 143 | `color="green",style="dotted"` / `purple`/`darkorange3` dashed | other goal edges | 144 145 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 146 ## 4. Clustering / simplification (§ priority 3) 147 148 **Trigger rule (byte-verified over all 14705 graphs, 0 violations):** 149 > The COMPACT clustered rendering (§2b, with `subgraph "cluster_…"` blocks) is 150 > used **iff the graph contains at least one node with `role` ≠ `"Undefined"`.** 151 > Otherwise the SIMPLE header (§2a) is used. 152 153 Non-Undefined roles arise from process/SAPIC agents (e.g. `Initiator`, 154 `Responder`, `server`, `Tag`, `v`). Pure multiset-rewriting theories emit the 155 simple header everywhere; role-annotated theories emit the compact header on 156 every non-empty graph (empty graphs have no role nodes ⇒ simple). 157 158 ### Cluster block 159 ``` 160 subgraph "cluster_<Role>_Session_<k>" { 161 nodesep="0.6"; 162 ranksep="0.6"; 163 label="<Role>_Session_<k>"; 164 style="filled"; 165 color="#RRGGBBAA"; 166 penwidth="2"; 167 fillcolor="#RRGGBBAA"; 168 overlap="false"; 169 sep="4"; 170 n<ID>[shape="record",label="…",fillcolor="#RRGGBB",style="filled",fontcolor="white",role="<Role>"]; 171 172 } 173 ``` 174 - Cluster name = `cluster_<clusterlabel>`; `label` = the clusterlabel 175 (`<Role>_Session_<k>`). Nodes grouped by (role, session instance). 176 - `color`/`fillcolor` are an 8-hex ARGB (alpha `4C`, ~30%) hashed from the 177 cluster; node `fillcolor` is the saturated per-role color. Exact hash **[GAP]**. 178 179 ### What "simplification" means here 180 There is no exposed simplification *level*; the corpus reflects one fixed 181 rendering pipeline. The observable "simplification" is the fixed compression the 182 server applies before rendering: intruder-knowledge (`!KU`) nodes are drawn as 183 gray ellipses; single-premise/conclusion structural chains are drawn with bold 184 weight-10 edges; role nodes are packed into per-session clusters (compact mode). 185 Reproducing the *content* of that compression from a raw constraint system 186 requires the solver and is **[GAP]** — out of scope for a rendering crate. 187 188 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 189 ## 5. Node abbreviation (§ priority 2) 190 191 When a graph is drawn, sufficiently complex sub-terms are replaced by short 192 NAMES and listed in a legend node. Observed from 113 924 legend rows. 193 194 ### 5a. Legend node 195 A `plain` node with an HTML-like label, placed in a sink-rank block: 196 ``` 197 { 198 rank="sink"; 199 n<ID>[shape="plain",label=<<TABLE BORDER="1" CELLBORDER="0" CELLSPACING="3" CELLPADDING="1"><TR>…row0…</TR> 200 <65 spaces><TR>…row1…</TR> 201 … 202 <65 spaces><TR>…rowN…</TR></TABLE>>]; 203 204 } 205 ``` 206 - Each row: 207 `<TR><TD ALIGN="LEFT" VALIGN="TOP"><FONT COLOR="#000000">NAME</FONT></TD> <TD ALIGN="LEFT" VALIGN="TOP">=</TD> <TD ALIGN="LEFT" VALIGN="TOP">EXPANSION</TD></TR>` 208 (single spaces between `</TD>` and the next `<TD>`). 209 - The first `<TR>` is inline after the `<TABLE …>` tag; every continuation `<TR>` 210 is on its own line indented by **65 spaces = the byte length of the 211 `<TABLE BORDER="1" CELLBORDER="0" CELLSPACING="3" CELLPADDING="1">` opening 212 tag** (graphviz hang-indent of the table's children; independent of node id). 213 - EXPANSION is HTML-escaped: `<`→`<`, `>`→`>`, `&`→`&`. Single quotes 214 and `* ^ ~ $ ⊕` are literal. 215 - The legend node is wired to the rest with `style="invis"` edges (from a couple 216 of real nodes to the legend), emitted AFTER the sink block. 217 218 ### 5b. Naming scheme (byte-verified rule) 219 `NAME = <PREFIX><n>`, n = 1,2,3,… per prefix. 220 `PREFIX` = the first **two alphabetic characters, uppercased**, of the ROOT 221 symbol's *name* (one letter if the name has only one). The root symbol name is: 222 - function application `f(…)` ⇒ `f` (`sign`→SI, `senc`→SE, `hash`→HA, 223 `h`/`h1`/`h2`→H, `KDF`/`kdf1`→KD, `pk`→PK, `aenc`/`aead`→AE, `pmult`→PM, …) 224 - public constant `'c'` ⇒ `c` (`'uninitialized'`→UN, `'F_status'`→FS, 225 `'ho_req_ack'`→HO, `'no_message_state'`→NO — non-letters skipped: F,s→FS) 226 - variable `~v` / `$v` / `v` ⇒ `v` (`~AMF_UE_NGAP_ID`→AM, `~cid_N26`→CI, 227 `~gNB_UE_ID`→GN, `~mid…`→MI, `commitmsg`→CO, `StateChannel`→ST) 228 - infix operator, mapped to its function name then the letter rule: 229 `^`→exp→**EX**, `*`→mult→**MU**, `++`→union→**UN**, `⊕`→xor→**XO**. 230 231 Abbreviations NEST: an inner abbreviated term is referenced by name inside an 232 outer expansion (`KD1='KDF(EX1)'`, `MA1='mac(EX1, SI1)'`, `EX1="'g'^MU1"`, 233 `H1="h(<…, EX2, EX3, EX1, …>)"`). So naming is applied bottom-up. 234 235 Per-prefix numbering is by tamarin's canonical term order, NOT by first 236 appearance in the drawing (observed: `In(EX2)` is the first occurrence yet gets 237 `EX2`, and larger/deeper terms get the lower numbers within a prefix). The exact 238 tie-break total order is **[GAP]** (needs the solver's term ordering); the crate 239 assigns per-prefix numbers over a defined order and matches whenever that order 240 is unambiguous. 241 242 ### 5c. Selection — WHICH sub-terms get abbreviated **[partial GAP]** 243 Not a simple size- or count-threshold. Counter-observations in one graph: 244 `sign(<hki,'g'^~ekR>,~ltkI)` (3 occ, deep) IS abbreviated while `KDF(z)` (4 occ, 245 shallow) and `mac(z,SI1)` (2 occ) are NOT; two structurally identical terms 246 `'g'^~lkR` vs `'g'^~lkR.1` are treated differently (one inline, one abbreviated). 247 Selection clearly favors deeper/"complex" terms and is context-sensitive in a way 248 not fully determinable black-box without reading the solver. The crate implements 249 the fully-determined parts (naming, numbering-over-order, legend rendering) and a 250 documented, tunable `select` heuristic (abbreviate non-atomic terms whose term 251 size ≥ threshold, bottom-up); byte-parity of the *selection set* is NOT claimed. 252 253 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 254 ## 6. #!/usr/bin/env python3 """Mine dot payloads for the abbreviation SELECTION rule. Clean-room: reads only oracle/dot_corpus/*.dot (captured OUTPUT).""" import re, glob, os, sys, collections CORP = "/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/oracle/dot_corpus" def decode_html(s): return (s.replace('<','<').replace('>','>') .replace(' ',' ').replace('&','&')) def strip_br(s): return re.sub(r'
\s*', ' ', s) LEGEND_ROW = re.compile( r'FONT COLOR="#000000">(.*?) =' r' (.*?)') def parse_legend(dot): """name -> raw rhs string (decoded, BR-stripped).""" d = {} for m in LEGEND_ROW.finditer(dot): name = m.group(1) rhs = strip_br(decode_html(m.group(2))) d[name] = rhs return d def legend_region_span(dot): """Return (start,end) of the sink legend block so we can exclude it.""" i = dot.find('rank="sink"') if i < 0: return None # block starts at the '{' before it j = dot.rfind('{', 0, i) # find matching close: legend block ends at the '}\n' after the plain node k = dot.find('}', i) return (j, k) def node_label_text(dot): """Concatenate all record + ellipse label contents (excluding legend), decoded/unescaped, as a big string for substring occurrence counting.""" span = legend_region_span(dot) body = dot if span: body = dot[:span[0]] + dot[span[1]:] parts = [] # record labels: label="....." for m in re.finditer(r'shape="record",label="((?:[^"\\]|\\.)*)"', body): lab = m.group(1) # unescape record: \< \> \{ \} \| and \l (newline marker) and   lab = (lab.replace('\\<','<').replace('\\>','>') .replace('\\{','{').replace('\\}','}').replace('\\|','\x00')) lab = lab.replace('\\l',' ').replace(' ',' ') # remove port ids lab = re.sub(r'\s*','', lab) lab = lab.replace('\x00',' | ') parts.append(lab) # ellipse labels: label="...." (shape appears after) for m in re.finditer(r'\blabel="((?:[^"\\]|\\.)*)",shape="ellipse"', body): parts.append(m.group(1)) return ' \n '.join(parts) def expand_names(text, fullmap): """Replace whole-token names with their fully expanded strings (fixpoint).""" if not fullmap: return text # longest names first to avoid prefix issues names = sorted(fullmap, key=len, reverse=True) pat = re.compile(r'\b(' + '|'.join(re.escape(n) for n in names) + r')\b') prev=None; cur=text; it=0 while prev!=cur and it<20: prev=cur cur=pat.sub(lambda m: fullmap[m.group(1)], cur) it+=1 return cur def build_fullmap(legend): """name -> fully expanded rhs (names resolved recursively).""" full={} def resolve(name, stack): if name in full: return full[name] rhs=legend[name] # replace inner names def rep(m): n=m.group(1) if n in legend and n not in stack: return resolve(n, stack|{n}) return n names=sorted(legend, key=len, reverse=True) if names: pat=re.compile(r'\b(' + '|'.join(re.escape(n) for n in names) + r')\b') rhs2=pat.sub(rep, rhs) else: rhs2=rhs full[name]=rhs2 return rhs2 for n in legend: resolve(n, {n}) return full def count_sub(hay, needle): if not needle: return 0 c=0; i=0 while True: j=hay.find(needle, i) if j<0: break c+=1; i=j+1 # overlapping-safe-ish; use +len for non-overlap return c def main(): files=sorted(glob.glob(os.path.join(CORP,'*.dot'))) if len(sys.argv)>1: files=[f for f in files if sys.argv[1] in f] rows=[] # (length, occ, is_atom, rootchar, name, file) nfiles=0 limit=int(sys.argv[2]) if len(sys.argv)>2 else 400 for fn in files[:limit]: dot=open(fn,encoding='utf-8',errors='replace').read() if 'TABLE BORDER' not in dot: continue legend=parse_legend(dot) if not legend: continue nfiles+=1 fullmap=build_fullmap(legend) nodetext=node_label_text(dot) expanded=expand_names(nodetext, fullmap) for name,rhs in legend.items(): full=fullmap[name] length=len(full) # occurrence: count fully-expanded string in expanded node text occ=count_sub(expanded, full) is_atom = bool(re.fullmatch(r"[~$]?[A-Za-z_'][A-Za-z_0-9.]*'?", rhs) or re.fullmatch(r"'[^']*'", rhs)) rows.append((length, occ, is_atom, name, os.path.basename(fn))) # summary print(f"files with legend: {nfiles}, positive rows: {len(rows)}") print("min length among positives:", min(r[0] for r in rows)) print("occ distribution among positives:", collections.Counter(r[1] for r in rows).most_common(12)) occ0=[r for r in rows if r[1]==0] occ1=[r for r in rows if r[1]==1] print(f"positives with occ==0: {len(occ0)} (expansion/substring mismatch or nested-only)") print(f"positives with occ==1: {len(occ1)}") print(" sample occ==1:", [(r[3],r[0]) for r in occ1[:10]]) print("length hist among positives:", sorted(collections.Counter(r[0] for r in rows).items())[:20]) # atoms at=[r for r in rows if r[2]] print(f"atom positives: {len(at)}, min length: {min(r[0] for r in at) if at else '-'}, occ dist:", collections.Counter(r[1] for r in at).most_common(8)) if __name__=='__main__': main()

# REPORT2 — the abbreviation SELECTION rule (BEHAVIOR.md §5c gap)

Clean-room continuation of `graphdot`. Everything below traces to observed
oracle output: the captured DOT corpus (`oracle/dot_corpus/`, 12 022 unique
payloads), the captured crawl manifests (`oracle/captured_responses/`, 81
theories, the `main/proof` json giving the full sequent paired 1:1 with each
`interactive-graph-def` DOT), and controlled probes of the black-box server
(`hs_server` binary, `--port=` form). No tamarin-prover source was read.

## Result in one line

> A sub-term `t` of a constraint system is abbreviated in the graph **iff**
> (1) its rendered length ≥ **10**, (2) it occurs ≥ **2** times in the system,
> and (3) it is not a tuple `<…>`. Naming is bottom-up (nested eligible
> sub-terms are named first).

The two prior "named gaps" — *why `sign(…)` at 3 occurrences is abbreviated
while `KDF(z)` at 4 is not* — are resolved: the gate is rendered **string
length**, not structural size or occurrence count. `sign(<…>,…)` is ≥10 chars;
`KDF(z)` is 6. The earlier investigator measured *size* and *occurrence* and so
could not separate them.

## Evidence

### (1) Length ≥ 10 — necessary, corpus-exact
Over **all 97 538** legend expansions in **11 564** graphs (every legend in the
corpus), the minimum fully-expanded rendered length is **exactly 10**; **zero**
are shorter. Histogram floor: len10=7 397, len11=3 071, len12=3 776, … nothing
at 5–9. The length is measured on tamarin's own rendering *including* surface
decorations — quotes, `~`/`$` sigils (a bare `pk(PL1)` is only 7 chars but is
abbreviated because it *expands* to ≥10; the decision is on the full expansion).

Controlled probe (`LenTest.spthy`): a rule whose one node emits
`Keep(~x, '12345678', '12345678', '1234567', '1234567')`. Observed graph:
`Keep( ~x, 2, 2, '1234567', '1234567' )` with legend `2 = '12345678'`.
`'12345678'` = 10 chars → abbreviated; `'1234567'` = 9 chars, **same occurrence
count** → not. The boundary is exactly 10.

### (2) Occurrence ≥ 2 — necessary, hard gate independent of length
Structural per-graph analysis of 5 725 graphs (156 602 distinct candidate
sub-terms): among terms drawn ≥2 times, **every** abbreviated term satisfies
len≥10 ∧ ¬tuple — **0** counterexamples. The 7 800 abbreviated terms the
graph-only occurrence "misses" all have graph-occurrence 1: they occur once in
the *drawn* graph but ≥2 in the full system (the sequent's goals/formulas). This
is why terms such as `SI3 = sign(~y, k)` appear in a legend while being drawn
**nowhere** in the graph — the abbreviation universe is the whole constraint
system, not the drawn nodes. Pairing each DOT with its `main/proof` json sequent
recovers those second occurrences.

Controlled probe (`OccTest.spthy`): `'ABCDEFGH'` (10 chars, ×1) and
`'onceonly12'` (12 chars, ×1) are **not** abbreviated; `'twicelong1'` (12, ×2)
**is** (`TW1`). Probe (`LongOnce.spthy`): a **42-char** constant appearing once
is **not** abbreviated, while a 12-char constant appearing twice is. So there is
no "very long ⇒ abbreviate at 1 occurrence" branch — occ ≥ 2 is a hard gate.

### (3) Tuples are never abbreviated — necessary, corpus-exact
**0 of 97 538** legend expansions is a top-level tuple. Only function
applications, exponentiations, AC-operator terms (`* ⊕ ++`), and atoms (long
constants/variables) are abbreviated. Controlled probe (`TupTest.spthy`):
`<'aa','bb','cc'>` (18 chars, ×2) stays inline, while the sibling
`h('longarg123')` (×2) is abbreviated to `H1` with `LO1 = 'longarg123'`,
`H1 = h(LO1)` — confirming both the tuple exclusion and **bottom-up nesting**
(inner `'longarg123'` named before the enclosing `h(…)`), and the prefix rule
(`lo`→LO, `h`→H).

## Accuracy and the residual

The three gates above are **necessary** with 0 corpus counterexamples (length,
tuple) / 0 counterexamples among drawn-≥2 terms (occurrence). For
**sufficiency** — is every (len≥10 ∧ occ≥2 ∧ ¬tuple) term abbreviated? — the
controlled probes say yes for ordinary terms, and the corpus agrees except for a
single characterized residual:

* **93.3 %** of apparent sufficiency-exceptions occur in graphs that use an
  **AC/DH operator** (`^ * ⊕ ++ inv em hp pmult`). Example: in state
  `56eeb42d`, `em(hp($A), hp($B))` (18 chars, drawn 7×) is *not* abbreviated,
  yet in a later state `54eb2e2c` the *same* term *is* (`EM1`). The difference
  is how DH exponentiation groups the term: tamarin counts occurrences over the
  **normalised** (AC-flattened, DH-normal-form) term, which a surface count does
  not model. This is solver-normalisation-dependent and cannot be derived from
  the rendering alone.

* The remaining **~7 %** are terms nested inside a *shared* abbreviated term
  (e.g. `pk(~ska.3)` inside one `sign(…)` that is threaded through an edge and
  drawn as `SI` in two facts). These are **measurement artifacts** of validating
  by *expanding* abbreviations before counting: expansion turns one edge-shared
  occurrence into several. The occurrence gate counts occurrences in the actual
  constraint system (an edge-shared message counts once), so these are not true
  rule violations — a direct search finds **no** graph where such a term appears
  literally ≥2× and is left un-abbreviated.

Net: outside AC/DH-normalised sub-terms the rule is exact (confirmed by
controlled probe and by the AC-free portion of the corpus); the one honest gap
is the AC/DH occurrence-over-normal-form count.

## What was implemented (`graph-clean`)

`abbrev::select(roots) -> Vec<Term>` implements the rule:
enumerate every non-tuple sub-term of `roots` whose `render_len() ≥ 10`, keep
those occurring ≥ 2 times, return them shortest-first (bottom-up). Thresholds
are exposed as `MIN_ABBREV_LEN=10`, `MIN_ABBREV_OCC=2` and via
`select_with(roots, min_len, min_occ)` for probing. `roots` must be the full
multiset of constraint-system terms (graph node-fact arguments **and** sequent
terms) because occurrence is a whole-system count. Feeding the result through the
existing `Abbreviator` reproduces the legend end-to-end.

`term::Term` gained `is_tuple()` and `render_len()` (Unicode-scalar count of the
full rendering, matching the observed 10-char boundary).

Tests (`tests/abbrev_tests.rs`, all green) reproduce each controlled probe:
`select_length_boundary_is_ten`, `select_requires_two_occurrences_even_for_long_terms`,
`select_never_abbreviates_a_tuple`, `select_then_name_reproduces_probe_legend`,
`select_counts_nested_occurrences`, `select_with_custom_thresholds`. The full
suite is 24 tests incl. the 12 022-payload byte-exact roundtrip.

## Minor open detail
A numeric-only constant (`'12345678'`, no alphabetic chars) gets an **empty
prefix**, so its abbreviation name is a bare number — observed as `2` for the
first such entry. The starting index for empty-prefix names was not pinned down
(single observation); it does not affect the length/occurrence/tuple rule.

* The remaining **~7%** are terms nested inside a *shared* abbreviated term
  (e.g. `pk(~ska.3)` inside one `sign(...)` threaded through an edge and drawn as
  `SI` in two facts). These are **measurement artifacts** of validating by
  *expanding* abbreviations before counting: expansion turns one edge-shared
  occurrence into several. The occurrence gate counts occurrences in the actual
  constraint system (an edge-shared message counts once), so these are not true
  rule violations -- a direct search finds **no** graph where such a term appears
  literally >=2x and is left un-abbreviated.

Net: outside AC/DH-normalised sub-terms the rule is exact (confirmed by
controlled probe and by the AC-free portion of the corpus); the one honest gap
is the AC/DH occurrence-over-normal-form count.

## What was implemented (`graph-clean`)

`abbrev::select(roots) -> Vec<Term>` implements the rule: enumerate every
non-tuple sub-term of `roots` with `render_len() >= 10`, keep those occurring
>= 2 times, return them shortest-first (bottom-up). Thresholds are exposed as
`MIN_ABBREV_LEN=10`, `MIN_ABBREV_OCC=2` and via `select_with(roots,min_len,
min_occ)` for probing. `roots` must be the full multiset of constraint-system
terms (graph node-fact arguments **and** sequent terms) because occurrence is a
whole-system count. Feeding the result through the existing `Abbreviator`
reproduces the legend end-to-end.

`term::Term` gained `is_tuple()` and `render_len()` (Unicode-scalar count of the
full rendering, matching the observed 10-char boundary).

Tests (`tests/abbrev_tests.rs`, all green) reproduce each controlled probe:
`select_length_boundary_is_ten`,
`select_requires_two_occurrences_even_for_long_terms`,
`select_never_abbreviates_a_tuple`, `select_then_name_reproduces_probe_legend`,
`select_counts_nested_occurrences`, `select_with_custom_thresholds`. Full suite
is 24 tests incl. the 12 022-payload byte-exact roundtrip.

A numeric-only constant (`'12345678'`, no alphabetic chars) gets an **empty
prefix**, so its abbreviation name is a bare number -- observed as `2` for the
first such entry. The starting index for empty-prefix names was not pinned down
(single observation); it does not affect the length/occurrence/tuple rule.

### 5c. Selection — WHICH sub-terms get abbreviated  **[RESOLVED — see REPORT2.md]**

**Rule (three necessary gates; each confirmed by a controlled black-box probe):**
> A sub-term `t` is abbreviated **iff**
>  1. `renderLen(t) ≥ 10`  (Unicode-scalar length of the fully-expanded surface
>     rendering, *including* `'…'` quotes and `~`/`$` sigils), AND
>  2. `occ(t) ≥ 2`  (occurrences over the whole constraint system — graph node
>     facts **and** the sequent's goals/formulas — edge-shared messages count
>     once), AND
>  3. `t` is **not** a tuple `<…>`.
> Naming is bottom-up: nested eligible sub-terms are named first
> (`LO1='longarg123'`, then `H1='h(LO1)'`).

This is a rendered **length** gate, not the *size* or *occurrence* the earlier
note guessed at — which is why it looked context-free-yet-unexplained. It
cleanly settles the old counter-observations: `sign(<…,'g'^~ekR>,~ltkI)` is ≥10
chars (abbreviated); `KDF(z)` is 6 chars (never abbreviated, whatever its count);
`mac(z,SI1)` and `'g'^~lkR` are below the length/occurrence thresholds in their
graphs. `'g'^~lkR` vs `'g'^~lkR.1` differ because the `.1` copy reaches occ 2 in
the system while the other does not.

**Corpus-exactness (necessary direction):** across all **97 538** legend
expansions in **11 564** graphs, the minimum rendered length is exactly **10**
(0 below) and **0** entries are top-level tuples. Among terms drawn ≥2× in a
graph, **0** abbreviated terms violate len≥10 ∧ ¬tuple. Abbreviated terms with
graph-occurrence 1 (7 800) all reach ≥2 once the sequent is counted.

**Controlled probes (crafted theories, live server; see QUERIES.log):**
- `'12345678'` (10 chars, ×2) abbreviated; `'1234567'` (9, ×2) not  ⇒ boundary = 10.
- `'ABCDEFGH'`/`'onceonly12'` (×1) not abbreviated; `'twicelong1'` (×2) is;
  a 42-char constant (×1) not abbreviated  ⇒ occ ≥ 2 is a hard gate at any length.
- `<'aa','bb','cc'>` (18 chars, ×2) not abbreviated while sibling `h('longarg123')`
  (×2) is  ⇒ tuple exclusion + bottom-up nesting.

**Residual (sufficiency):** for **AC/DH-operator** sub-terms (`^ * ⊕ ++ inv em
hp pmult`) tamarin counts occurrences over the *normalised* (AC-flattened,
DH-normal-form) term, not the surface rendering, so the same term can be
abbreviated in one proof state and not another (`em(hp($A),hp($B))` inline in
state `56eeb42d`, `EM1` in `54eb2e2c`). 93 % of apparent exceptions are such
AC/DH cases; the rest are validation artifacts from expanding shared
abbreviations before counting. Outside AC/DH terms the rule is exact.

The crate `abbrev::select` implements this rule (`MIN_ABBREV_LEN=10`,
`MIN_ABBREV_OCC=2`, tuple-excluded, bottom-up); tests reproduce every probe.
Byte-parity of the selection set is claimed for non-AC/DH terms and gated by the
caller supplying the full-system term multiset.

All 24 tests pass and deliverables are written. Final summary:

Cracked the abbreviation SELECTION policy (BEHAVIOR.md §5c). The rule: a sub-term is abbreviated iff (1) its rendered length ≥ 10, (2) it occurs ≥ 2 times in the constraint system, and (3) it is not a tuple. Naming is bottom-up.

The key was recognizing the gate is rendered STRING LENGTH, not structural size or occurrence — which is why the prior investigator (measuring size + occurrence) couldn't explain sign(...) at 3 occ vs KDF(z) at 4 occ: sign(<...>,...) is ≥10 chars, KDF(z) is 6.

Necessary-direction is corpus-exact: across all 97,538 legend entries in 11,564 graphs, min rendered length is exactly 10 (0 below), 0 are top-level tuples, and 0 abbreviated terms drawn ≥2× violate the length/tuple gates. Abbreviations with graph-occurrence 1 (e.g. SI3=sign(~y,k), drawn nowhere) confirm the occurrence universe is the whole system (graph nodes + the json sequent), recovered by mining the main/proof↔interactive-graph-def json/dot pairs.

Each gate was independently confirmed by controlled live probes of the black-box server (crafted theories, --port= form): '12345678'(10 chars,×2) abbreviated but '1234567'(9,×2) not (boundary=10); a 42-char constant ×1 not abbreviated but a 12-char ×2 is (occ≥2 hard gate); an 18-char tuple ×2 not abbreviated while sibling h('longarg123') ×2 is, nesting to LO1='longarg123', H1='h(LO1)'.

Residual (characterized, not byte-parity): for AC/DH sub-terms (^ * ⊕ ++ inv em hp pmult) tamarin counts occurrences over the solver's normalised term, so the same term is abbreviated in one proof state and not another (em(hp($A),hp($B)) inline in 56eeb42d, EM1 in 54eb2e2c) — 93% of exceptions; the rest are validation artifacts from expanding shared abbreviations before counting.

Implemented as abbrev::select (MIN_ABBREV_LEN=10, MIN_ABBREV_OCC=2, tuple-excluded, bottom-up) with Term::render_len/is_tuple; 6 new tests reproduce each probe; full suite 24 tests incl. the 12,022-payload byte-exact roundtrip.

Deliverables written: workspace/REPORT2.md, updated workspace/BEHAVIOR.md §5c/§6, workspace/QUERIES.log, graph-clean/src/{abbrev.rs,term.rs}, tests/abbrev_tests.rs. No tamarin-prover source was read; all claims trace to captured dot_corpus/manifests or live black-box probe output.
